Appearances (via zoom videoconference):
Atty. RICHARD TAGUINOD, Esq. (tel: 925-963-1487) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SURESH GUNASEKARAN, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, NADER NAJAFI, MEGAN BETANCOURT, AND TYRONE JOHNSON's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

The Court notes defendants' counsel (contesting party) is not present. The Court adopts its tentative ruling, as the contesting party failed to appear.

Defendants' motion to strike parts of the complaint has two grounds. First, defendants say the "claims for Elder Abuse [sic] are subject to demurrer," so enhanced remedies are "non-recoverable." (Mot. 2:13-15.) But the demurrer has been overruled, so this part of the motion is denied. Second, defendants rightly note that the complaint's prayer for $1.95 million in non-economic damages (id. at 25:11) runs afoul of Civil Code 3333.2(f), so this part of the motion is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via zoom videoconference):
Atty. RICHARD TAGUINOD, Esq. (tel: 925-963-1487) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SURESH GUNASEKARAN, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, NADER NAJAFI, MEGAN BETANCOURT, AND TYRONE JOHNSON's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The Court notes defendants' counsel (contesting party) is not present. The Court adopts its tentative ruling, as the contesting party failed to appear.

Defendants' demurrer to count 6 of the complaint is overruled. The count specifically pleads the elements of abuse of a dependent adult under a neglect theory. (CACI 3103; Cmplt. 19:24-24:20.) Deliberate disregard is not an element of this cause of action, as defendants assert. (Dmr. 2:6-11; CACI 3103.) Whether the complaint adequately pleads enhanced remedies (CACI 3104) is not directly addressed by the demurrer; in any event, on general demurrer the test is whether the complaint states any valid claim and this one does. (See Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
